name: Jalyn Gearries
school: School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems
focus: Remote Sensing • Riparian vegetation • Invasive species monitoring
bio: Jalyn Gearries is a PhD student in the School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems at Northern Arizona University whose research focuses on remote sensing applications for ecosystem monitoring in the Grand Canyon. Her work uses high-resolution satellite imagery and geospatial analysis to evaluate riparian vegetation change and invasive species treatments along the Colorado River, in partnership with the National Park Service.
